eviction.court_process_summary_rule: {"statutes":"T.C.A. §§ 66-28-501, 66-28-502, 66-28-505, 66-28-507, 66-28-509","notice_requirements":{"nonpayment_of_rent":"14-day notice to pay or vacate (T.C.A. § 66-28-505)","holdover_tenant":"30-day notice to terminate for month-to-month tenancy","lease_violation":"30-day notice to cure or quit for noncompliance (T.C.A. § 66-28-507)","no_cause":"30-day notice for at-will tenancy (T.C.A. § 66-28-501)","unlawful_detainer":"Notice and court action required under T.C.A. § 29-18-101 et seq."},"full_text":"T.C.A. § 66-28-505. Failure to pay rent. (a) If rent is due and unpaid, the landlord may give written notice to the tenant requiring the tenant to pay rent or vacate the premises within fourteen (14) days after the delivery of the notice to the tenant. (b) The notice shall specify that the tenant has fourteen (14) days to pay rent or vacate. If the tenant fails to pay rent or vacate within the fourteen-day period, the landlord may bring an action for possession and rent.\nT.C.A. § 66-28-507. Noncompliance. (a) If the tenant materially fails to comply with the rental agreement or fails to fulfill the tenant's obligations under § 66-28-401, the landlord may give written notice specifying the tenant's acts or omissions and stating that the rental agreement will terminate upon a date not less than thirty (30) days after receipt of the notice. (b) If the failure is remediable and the tenant adequately remedies the breach within thirty (30) days after receipt of the notice, the rental agreement does not terminate. (c) If the breach is not remediable by repair or the payment of damages, the landlord may give written notice specifying the breach and stating that the rental agreement will terminate upon a date not less than fourteen (14) days after receipt of the notice."}

{"statutes":"T.C.A. §§ 66-28-301, 66-28-302","full_text":"T.C.A. § 66-28-301. Security deposit.
(a) Upon request by the landlord for a tenant to vacate or within five (5) days after receipt by the landlord of written notice of the tenant's intent to vacate, the landlord shall notify the tenant of the tenant's right to be present at the landlord's inspection of the premises to determine the tenant's liability for physical damages that are the basis for any charge against the security deposit and the landlord's estimated costs to cure such damages.
(b) The landlord shall conduct the inspection within a reasonable time after the tenant vacates. If the tenant desires to be present at the inspection, the tenant shall notify the landlord, and the landlord shall conduct a joint inspection with the tenant and compile a comprehensive listing of any presently observable damage to the premises. Both the landlord and the tenant shall sign the listing and shall receive a copy. Such signed listing shall be conclusive evidence of the accuracy of the listing in any subsequent dispute regarding the security deposit.
(c) If the landlord conducts the inspection without the tenant present: (i) If the tenant has not notified the landlord of the tenant's intent to be present, the landlord may proceed without the tenant; (ii) If the tenant has abandoned the premises; (iii) If a court orders the tenant to vacate; (iv) If the tenant notifies the landlord that the tenant does not wish to be present; (v) If the tenant fails to appear for a scheduled inspection; or (vi) If the tenant does not request to be present.
(d) If the landlord fails to comply with this section by not providing a checklist of damages, the landlord is prohibited from recovering any portion of the security deposit as damages.
(e) Disposition. If the tenant owes no rent or other charges and there is a refund due the tenant, the landlord shall deliver to the tenant, by first class mail to the tenant's last known address, a refund and a written statement itemizing the basis for, and describing the condition and circumstances of, any damages and the estimated cost to cure the damages. If the tenant does not respond within sixty (60) days after the delivery of the statement, the landlord may retain the deposit.
(f) Additional damages. If additional damages are discovered within thirty (30) days after the tenant vacates or within seven (7) days after a new tenant moves in, whichever is earlier, the landlord may recover the cost of repairs from the tenant.
(g) Disclosure. At the time of execution of the rental agreement and delivery of the deposit, the landlord shall disclose to the tenant the location of the account in which the deposit is held, but is not required to disclose the account number."}

{"statutes":"T.C.A. §§ 66-28-301, 66-28-302","full_text":"T.C.A. § 66-28-301. Security deposit.
(a) Upon request by the landlord for a tenant to vacate or within five (5) days after receipt by the landlord of written notice of the tenant's intent to vacate, the landlord shall notify the tenant of the tenant's right to be present at the landlord's inspection of the premises to determine the tenant's liability for physical damages that are the basis for any charge against the security deposit and the landlord's estimated costs to cure such damages.
(b) The landlord shall conduct the inspection within a reasonable time after the tenant vacates. If the tenant desires to be present at the inspection, the tenant shall notify the landlord, and the landlord shall conduct a joint inspection with the tenant and compile a comprehensive listing of any presently observable damage to the premises. Both the landlord and the tenant shall sign the listing and shall receive a copy. Such signed listing shall be conclusive evidence of the accuracy of the listing in any subsequent dispute regarding the security deposit.
(c) If the landlord conducts the inspection without the tenant present: (i) If the tenant has not notified the landlord of the tenant's intent to be present, the landlord may proceed without the tenant; (ii) If the tenant has abandoned the premises; (iii) If a court orders the tenant to vacate; (iv) If the tenant notifies the landlord that the tenant does not wish to be present; (v) If the tenant fails to appear for a scheduled inspection; or (vi) If the tenant does not request to be present.
(d) If the landlord fails to comply with this section by not providing a checklist of damages, the landlord is prohibited from recovering any portion of the security deposit as damages.
(e) Disposition. If the tenant owes no rent or other charges and there is a refund due the tenant, the landlord shall deliver to the tenant, by first class mail to the tenant's last known address, a refund and a written statement itemizing the basis for, and describing the condition and circumstances of, any damages and the estimated cost to cure the damages. If the tenant does not respond within sixty (60) days after the delivery of the statement, the landlord may retain the deposit.
(f) Additional damages. If additional damages are discovered within thirty (30) days after the tenant vacates or within seven (7) days after a new tenant moves in, whichever is earlier, the landlord may recover the cost of repairs from the tenant.
(g) Disclosure. At the time of execution of the rental agreement and delivery of the deposit, the landlord shall disclose to the tenant the location of the account in which the deposit is held, but is not required to disclose the account number."}

(a) Upon request by the landlord for a tenant to vacate or within five (5) days after receipt by the landlord of written notice of the tenant's intent to vacate, the landlord shall notify the tenant of the tenant's right to be present at the landlord's inspection of the premises to determine the tenant's liability for physical damages that are the basis for any charge against the security deposit and the landlord's estimated costs to cure such damages.
(b) The landlord shall conduct the inspection within a reasonable time after the tenant vacates. If the tenant desires to be present at the inspection, the tenant shall notify the landlord, and the landlord shall conduct a joint inspection with the tenant and compile a comprehensive listing of any presently observable damage to the premises. Both the landlord and the tenant shall sign the listing and shall receive a copy. Such signed listing shall be conclusive evidence of the accuracy of the listing in any subsequent dispute regarding the security deposit.
(c) If the landlord conducts the inspection without the tenant present: (i) If the tenant has not notified the landlord of the tenant's intent to be present, the landlord may proceed without the tenant; (ii) If the tenant has abandoned the premises; (iii) If a court orders the tenant to vacate; (iv) If the tenant notifies the landlord that the tenant does not wish to be present; (v) If the tenant fails to appear for a scheduled inspection; or (vi) If the tenant does not request to be present.
(d) If the landlord fails to comply with this section by not providing a checklist of damages, the landlord is prohibited from recovering any portion of the security deposit as damages.
(e) Disposition. If the tenant owes no rent or other charges and there is a refund due the tenant, the landlord shall deliver to the tenant, by first class mail to the tenant's last known address, a refund and a written statement itemizing the basis for, and describing the condition and circumstances of, any damages and the estimated cost to cure the damages. If the tenant does not respond within sixty (60) days after the delivery of the statement, the landlord may retain the deposit.
(f) Additional damages. If additional damages are discovered within thirty (30) days after the tenant vacates or within seven (7) days after a new tenant moves in, whichever is earlier, the landlord may recover the cost of repairs from the tenant.
(g) Disclosure. At the time of execution of the rental agreement and delivery of the deposit, the landlord shall disclose to the tenant the location of the account in which the deposit is held, but is not required to disclose the account number."}

sources.title: {"statutes":"T.C.A. §§ 66-28-501, 66-28-502, 66-28-505, 66-28-507, 66-28-509","notice_requirements":{"nonpayment_of_rent":"14-day notice to pay or vacate (T.C.A. § 66-28-505)","holdover_tenant":"30-day notice to terminate for month-to-month tenancy","lease_violation":"30-day notice to cure or quit for noncompliance (T.C.A. § 66-28-507)","no_cause":"30-day notice for at-will tenancy (T.C.A. § 66-28-501)","unlawful_detainer":"Notice and court action required under T.C.A. § 29-18-101 et seq."},"full_text":"T.C.A. § 66-28-505. Failure to pay rent. (a) If rent is due and unpaid, the landlord may give written notice to the tenant requiring the tenant to pay rent or vacate the premises within fourteen (14) days after the delivery of the notice to the tenant. (b) The notice shall specify that the tenant has fourteen (14) days to pay rent or vacate. If the tenant fails to pay rent or vacate within the fourteen-day period, the landlord may bring an action for possession and rent.\nT.C.A. § 66-28-507. Noncompliance. (a) If the tenant materially fails to comply with the rental agreement or fails to fulfill the tenant's obligations under § 66-28-401, the landlord may give written notice specifying the tenant's acts or omissions and stating that the rental agreement will terminate upon a date not less than thirty (30) days after receipt of the notice. (b) If the failure is remediable and the tenant adequately remedies the breach within thirty (30) days after receipt of the notice, the rental agreement does not terminate. (c) If the breach is not remediable by repair or the payment of damages, the landlord may give written notice specifying the breach and stating that the rental agreement will terminate upon a date not less than fourteen (14) days after receipt of the notice."}

sources.title: {"statutes":"T.C.A. § 66-28-403","full_text":"T.C.A. § 66-28-403. Access by landlord. (a) The tenant shall not unreasonably withhold consent to the landlord to enter the dwelling unit. (b) The landlord may enter the dwelling unit without consent of the tenant if: (1) The entry is at a reasonable time; (2) The landlord gives the tenant reasonable notice before entering; and (3) The entry is for one of the following purposes: (i) To inspect the premises; (ii) To make ordinary, necessary, or agreed repairs, decorations, alterations, or improvements; (iii) To supply necessary or agreed services; (iv) To exhibit the dwelling unit to prospective or actual purchasers, mortgagees, tenants, workmen, or contractors; (v) If the tenant is absent from the premises and the landlord reasonably believes that entry is necessary to protect the premises or property. (c) The landlord may enter without notice in the case of an emergency. (d) The landlord shall not abuse the right of access conferred by this section. (e) Reasonable notice for non-emergency entry is presumed to be at least twenty-four (24) hours."}

(b) The landlord may enter the dwelling unit without consent of the tenant if: (1) The entry is at a reasonable time; (2) The landlord gives the tenant reasonable notice before entering; and (3) The entry is for one of the following purposes: (i) To inspect the premises; (ii) To make ordinary, necessary, or agreed repairs, decorations, alterations, or improvements; (iii) To supply necessary or agreed services; (iv) To exhibit the dwelling unit to prospective or actual purchasers, mortgagees, tenants, workmen, or contractors; (v) If the tenant is absent from the premises and the landlord reasonably believes that entry is necessary to protect the premises or property. (c) The landlord may enter without notice in the case of an emergency. (d) The landlord shall not abuse the right of access conferred by this section. (e) Reasonable notice for non-emergency entry is presumed to be at least twenty-four (24) hours."}

sources.title: {"statutes":"T.C.A. §§ 66-28-201, 66-28-202","full_text":"T.C.A. § 66-28-201. Landlord's obligations. (a) The landlord shall: (1) Comply with all applicable building and housing code requirements that materially affect health and safety; (2) Make all repairs and do whatever is necessary to put and keep the premises in a fit and habitable condition; (3) Keep all common areas of the premises in a clean and safe condition; (4) Maintain in good and safe working order all electrical, plumbing, sanitary, heating, ventilating, air conditioning, and other facilities and appliances, including elevators, provided by the landlord; (5) Provide and maintain appropriate garbage containers and arrange for garbage removal in the case of two (2) or more dwelling units; (6) Supply running water and reasonable amounts of hot water at all times and reasonable heat unless the heat hot water is generated by a building heating or water-heating system under the tenant's exclusive control; (7) Not engage in or permit any drug-related criminal activity as defined in § 40-39-111 on the premises.\nT.C.A. § 66-28-202. Implied warranty of habitability. In addition to the obligations imposed by § 66-28-201, there is an implied warranty of habitability that the premises are and will remain fit for human habitation. This warranty cannot be waived."}

Implied warranty of habitability. In addition to the obligations imposed by § 66-28-201, there is an implied warranty of habitability that the premises are and will remain fit for human habitation. This warranty cannot be waived."}
